items.title: VI Live: FC Utrecht verdedigt tegen AZ, Chelsea scoort uit corner tegen Arsenal
items.summary: Zondag staan er liefst vijf Eredivisie-duels op de rol. Ajax en Feyenoord hebben een apart liveblog. Hier volg je onder meer de ontmoeting tussen FC Utrecht en AZ. Arsenal ontvangt Chelsea en in Italië staan AS Roma en Juventus tegenover elkaar. Volg het in de VI Live.

items.title: Twente dankt opmars aan fans en beste verdediging van Eredivisie
items.summary: De lach is terug bij FC Twente. Na de fraaie zege op Feyenoord kunnen de Tukkers omhoog kijken in de Eredivisie, met een goede vorm, 'geweldige' supporters en de minste tegengoals van de hele competitie.

items.title: Arsenal v Chelsea: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 4.30pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail Scott 4 min: A fairly shapeless start, actually. “The best pints are Thursday night.” Hello Ian McCourt, formerly of this parish. “Or about 1.30pm on a Friday.” 2 min: A mid-octane start to the game. No real shape yet, though it’s obvious that Palmer is prowling the left wing today, opposite to his usual beat. Continue reading...

items.title: Valente beleeft frustrerende middag met Feyenoord: 'Dat is iets mentaals'
items.summary: Luciano Valente heeft moeite met de manier waarop Feyenoord zondagmiddag werd afgetroefd door FC Twente. De middenvelder van de Rotterdammers zag dat zijn ploeg onder meer tekortkwam bij het winnen van tweede ballen. Dat is iets mentaals, aldus Valente.

items.title: Kruys dolgelukkig na 'knotsgekke' zege: 'Robert Mühren is een fenomeen'
items.summary: FC Volendam sloeg zondag een grote slag in zijn strijd tegen degradatie , door diep in blessuretijd van FC Groningen te winnen. De Noord-Hollanders staan nu vijf punten voor op nummer zestien NAC Breda. Trainer Rick Kruys telde na afloop zijn zegeningen.

items.title: 'Hearts & Motherwell the winners after damaging Old Firm stalemate'
items.summary: BBC Scotland's chief sportswriter Tom English evaluates the impact of Sunday's draw at Ibrox.

items.title: Van Persie legt vinger op zere plek: 'Gevoelsmatig word je teruggeworpen'
items.summary: Feyenoord-trainer Robin van Persie voelt dat zijn ploeg is teruggeworpen na de nederlaag tegen FC Twente. Zijn ploeg was qua uitslagen bezig aan een goede serie, maar leed zondag een kansloze nederlaag.

items.title: Sesko streak continues as Manchester United fight off 10-man Crystal Palace
items.summary: As the second half began, a banner appeared in the Stretford End that read: “MUFC proudly colonised by immigrants.” If this was a riposte to Sir Jim Ratcliffe’s assertion that these shores have been overrun by those from overseas (for which the co-owner half-heartedly apologised), Manchester United needed their own reply to a listless opening period that left them trailing to Maxence Lacroix’s early header. Eleven minutes after the restart, they did. First came Lacroix’s sending-off, issued by Chris Kavanagh after a pitchside monitor review for yanking over Matheus Cunha. The contact started before the 18-yard line but it continued into the penalty area, so the referee followed up awarding a spot-kick by showing his red card. Fernandes calmly beat Dean Henderson to the left of the Crystal Palace No 1, who guessed wrong. Continue reading...

items.title: Wilson and Iwobi earn Fulham win to increase Tottenham relegation fears
items.summary: As each passing week goes by, the threat of relegation becomes more real for Tottenham. This is no longer a quirk that it can be assumed will automatically be corrected. The monster not only exists but is getting closer, and fear is beginning to set in and make itself seen in their play. Defeat on Sunday was not only far more emphatic than the scoreline might suggest, but it was their fourth reverse in a row, extending their run without a win in the Premier League to nine. The only sliver of relief was that all their relegation rivals lost as well. But this was grim stuff from Spurs. Igor Tudor had hoped the 4-1 defeat in the north London derby would be a wake-up call. But if anything, this was even worse. Arsenal won because they are better than Tottenham; Fulham won because Spurs were terrible. Tudor, the arch-firefighter, has a huge job on his hands. Continue reading...

items.title: Welbeck lifts Brighton to leave Nottingham Forest two points off drop zone
items.summary: These are concerning times for Vítor Pereira. Nottingham Forest’s fourth permanent manager of this crazy season may be only four matches into his spell in charge but – given owner Evangelos Marinakis’s record – his position is already looking precarious. A second Premier League defeat in succession since he was appointed to replace Sean Dyche a fortnight ago leaves Forest sweating just two points clear of the drop zone and facing a daunting trip to Manchester City on Wednesday. They never looked like capitalising on West Ham’s heavy defeat against Liverpool on Saturday against a Brighton side packed with experience and who recorded their first home victory for two months thanks to goals from Diego Gómez and Danny Welbeck – his 10th of the season – to ease more of the pressure on Fabian Hürzeler. Continue reading...

items.title: Sesko nets another winner in Man United comeback
items.summary: Manchester United came from a goal down to claim another morale-boosting victory over Crystal Palace on Sunday.

items.title: Pulisic's Milan score late double, 10 pts off Inter
items.summary: AC Milan needed two late goals to secure a 2-0 win at Cremonese in Serie A on Sunday and bounce back from its second loss of the season.

items.title: Qatar postpones all games; no news on Finalissima
items.summary: The Qatar football federation postponed all tournaments and matches until further notice on Sunday amid global tensions following the U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran.

items.title: Madrid 'clear' on Mbappé, but doubt for City clash
items.summary: Coach Álvaro Arbeloa said on Sunday that Real Madrid are "very clear" about Kylian Mbappé's knee problem but admitted he is a doubt to face Manchester City.

items.title: Spurs' relegation fears deepen with loss at Fulham
items.summary: Tottenham’s relegation fears deepened after a 2-1 defeat at Fulham equalled their longest Premier League winless run.

items.title: Man Utd into top three as Sesko completes turnaround against 10-man Palace
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko celebrated his first start under Michael Carrick with the winner as Man Utd came from behind to beat 10-man Crystal Palace 2-1 and move third for the first time since May 2023.

items.title: Röhl still believes in Rangers' title bid but Boyd backs Hearts
items.summary: A defiant Danny Röhl believes Rangers are not out of the Scottish Premiership title race, but Sky Sports' Kris Boyd is now backing Hearts for the top spot.

items.title: Premier League live updates: Arsenal face big title test in London derby vs. Chelsea
items.summary: null

items.title: Reo Hatate grabs Celtic a draw at Rangers to leave Hearts as real winners
items.summary: A penny for the thoughts of the Hearts manager, Derek McInnes, as John Beaton was diverted by the video assistant referee towards the pitchside screen. Only seconds of regulation time remained. An afternoon that for so long looked to belong to Rangers was about to take a significant twist. As Beaton awarded Celtic a ­penalty, from which Reo Hatate ­eventually scored, idle Hearts emerged the real winners. The Edinburgh club’s aspirations of winning the Scottish title for the first time since 1960 have been enhanced over back-to-back ­weekends. Hearts lead Rangers by six and Celtic, who play their game in hand at Aberdeen on Wednesday, by eight. McInnes has the making of ­history within his grasp. No wonder Sir Alex Ferguson, the last non‑Old Firm ­manager to win the top league, thought it ­appropriate to take in the weekend fixtures at Tynecastle and Ibrox. Continue reading...

items.title: Hatate rescues Celtic in Old Firm classic
items.summary: Reo Hatate scored a late equaliser to rescue a point for Celtic as they came from two goals down to draw 2-2 with Rangers in a pulsating Old Firm clash at Ibrox.
